title: gentiana verna subsp. pumila
intro: Gentiana verna subsp. pumila, also known as small alpine gentian, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the gentian family that typically grows in mountainous regions and is characterized by its small, blue flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Gentiana verna subsp. pumila include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antiviral and antifungal properties. Gentiana verna subsp. pumila can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
